book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: (Labbe and Cossart, Concilia, Tom.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And all the most reverend bishops at the same time cried out.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: This is a just judgment.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: To Cœlestine, a new Paul!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: To Cyril a new Paul!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: To Cœlestine the guardian of the faith!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: To Cœlestine of one mind with the synod!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: To Cœlestine the whole Synod offers its thanks!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: One Cœlestine!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: One faith of the Synod!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: One faith of the world!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Projectus, the most reverend bishop and legate, said: Let your holiness consider the form (τύπον) of the writings of the holy and venerable pope Cœlestine, the bishop, who has exhorted your holiness (not as if teaching the ignorant, but as reminding them that know) that those things which he had long ago defined, and now thought it right to remind you of, ye might give command to be carried out to the uttermost, according to the canon of the common faith, and according to the use of the Catholic Church.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Firmus, the bishop of Cæsarea in Cappadocia said: The Apostolic and holy see of the most holy bishop Cœlestine, hath previously given a decision and type (τύπον) in this matter, through the writings which were sent to the most God beloved bishops, to wit to Cyril of Alexandria, and to Juvenal of Jerusalem, and to Rufus of Thessalonica, and to the holy churches, both of Constantinople and of Antioch.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: This we have also followed and (since the limit set for Nestorius’s emendation was long gone by, and much time has passed since our arrival at the city of Ephesus in accordance with the decree of the most pious emperor, and thereupon having delayed no little time so that the day fixed by the emperor was past; and since Nestorius although cited had not appeared) we carried into effect the type (τύπον) having pronounced against him a canonical and apostolical judgment.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Arcadius the most reverend bishop and legate, said: Although our sailing was slow, and contrary winds hindered us especially, so that we did not know whether we should arrive at the destined place, as we had hoped, nevertheless by God’s good providence…Wherefore we desire to ask your blessedness, that you command that we be taught what has been already decreed by your holiness.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Philip, presbyter and legate of the Apostolic See said: We offer our thanks to the holy and venerable Synod, that when the writings of our holy and blessed pope had been read to you, the holy members by our [or your] holy voices, ye joined yourselves to the holy head also by your holy acclamations.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: For your blessedness is not ignorant that the head of the whole faith, the head of the Apostles, is blessed Peter the Apostle.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And since now our mediocrity, after having been tempest-tossed and much vexed, has arrived, we ask that ye give order that there be laid before us what things were done in this holy Synod before our arrival; in order that according to the opinion of our blessed pope and of this present holy assembly, we likewise may ratify their determination.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Theodotus, the bishop of Ancyra said: The God of the whole world has made manifest the justice of the judgment pronounced by the holy Synod by the writings of the most religious bishop Cœlestine, and by the coming of your holiness.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: For ye have made manifest the zeal of the most holy and reverend bishop Cœlestine, and his care for the pious faith.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And since very reasonably your reverence is desirous of learning what has been done from the minutes of the acts concerning the deposition of Nestorius your reverence will be fully convinced of the justice of the sentence, and of the zeal of the holy Synod, and the symphony of the faith which the most pious and holy bishop Cœlestine has proclaimed with a great voice, of course after your full conviction, the rest shall be added to the present action.
